tags.evidence: learn to welcome and attend
learn to welcome and attend
out of the underworld, toward the community
People gathered in Zuccotti Park
form that could hold that flow
refusing to wait for permission
communities of practice that meet every week
each one carrying what the underworld gave them
the governance that holds the field open
way your attention sits at this page
The angel pays attention
The council holds the seeing
the council has been gathered
reading what is in front of you
refuses to look away
discipline of seeing what carried through
ready to be welcomed or refused
conversation with the herons in your actual voice
you were trained to read it as empty
you name what is happening
while you read the room
hold what cannot be set down
behind the bar, polishing glasses
carrying had been shared
felt the weight come off my shoulders
There is a name I have for the work
way of being for other people to inhabit
I only had to not get in the way
to be paid attention to it
raise on the site you are on
things you do not get to build
I will not. It was never mine to give
name theirs in their own life
the one already there, declining to look at
come back to the next descent
Carry it as you walk
Share as much of it as the rooms
Where the others burned, he taught
gave up endless life so Prometheus could be freed
blessed the night herons in the willow oaks
become the land inviting people into relationship
closed a restaurant and lost his idealism
This one names the wound
hold the book's full intellectual architecture
finding the thread and pulling it through
the judgment that something corresponds to lived experience
keep governing, attending to whether the water reaches
attending to whether the water reaches the fields
I became the land inviting them into relationship
quiet the subtle movements of my body
watched children learn it in a week
I watched a tiny kinglet taking shelter
We tell them we love them and wish them good luck
We find the nests
see the trickle when the catastrophe trained you
training the attention so the trickle becomes visible
registers as something worth watering
look back at the people who did this before you
the people who bless the nests
they feel the quieted body that years of practice produced
telling the story proudly
trains the attention toward what is real and living
telling them you love them — this is political
Finding the heron nests
a practice to be continued
carry it forward without knowing
